strengths: Successfully improved Win Rate to 72.3% from the parent spec's 71.2% by tightening the threshold, validating the original feedback.
Maintained a strong, profitable signal with a positive aggregate expectancy of +3.75 bps over 155 trades.
Achieved an even higher average expectancy (+3.75 bps) compared to the parent spec (+2.93 bps), suggesting the more extreme signals are more profitable.
weaknesses: Tightening the threshold drastically reduced the number of trades from 1366 in the parent spec to just 155, an 88.6% decrease, which severely limits the strategy's applicability.
Performance is inconsistent across days and symbols, with daily Win Rates ranging from 67.4% (005930 on 20260326) to 78.4% (000660 on 20260323), an 11 percentage point spread.
Signal generation is sparse, with zero trades on one day (005930 on 20260317) and fewer than 30 trades on three other days, reducing the statistical confidence in daily performance metrics.
recommended_direction_reasoning: The spec confirms a candidate edge with WR=72.3% and expectancy=+3.75 bps over 155 trades. Tightening the threshold from 1.5 to 2.0 successfully increased both WR and expectancy. The next logical step is to continue this trend by tightening the threshold further (e.g., to 2.5) to see if an even higher-conviction subset of signals exists, which would fully characterize the signal's strength at its extremes.
